events.label: assistant
events.text:
Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. 

Bottom line: feeding downstream outlets off a single upstream GFCI receptacle (LINE/LOAD feed-through) is legitimate, code-compliant journeyman practice under 210.8 — but only when wired correctly and verified. The described condition (reversed LINE/LOAD, no downstream tester verification, missing required labeling, and unclear AFCI coverage on the extended run per 210.12) is the common shortcut version of this technique, leaving downstream receptacles unprotected while looking fine. That's a `fail`, high risk, with rework required before sign-off.
